title: Lemma 'geometric slices': undefined notion + asserted key equivalence

comments.author: human
comments.body:
In Lemma 2 (geometric slices), the term **'geometric Mackey functor $M$'** is never defined, yet the entire argument rests on it. The proof asserts that for any finite $G$-set $T$, the fixed-point inclusion $S^{|T/G|}\hookrightarrow N^T S^1$ induces an **equivalence** $S^{|T/G|}\otimes HM \xrightarrow{\sim} N^T S^1\otimes HM$. This is the crux of the converse and is stated without proof.

Since equivalences in $\mathrm{Sp}^G$ are detected by $\Phi^K$ for **all** $K\subseteq G$, one must check the equivalence on $\Phi^K$ for every $K$, not merely $K=G$. The proof only invokes $\Phi^G$. Moreover the bracket computation
$$[N^TS^1,\Sigma^kHM]^G\cong[\Phi^GN^TS^1,\Sigma^kHM(G/G)]\cong[S^{|T/G|},\Sigma^kHM(G/G)]$$
is itself nontrivial: it requires $HM$ to be concentrated over $G/G$ (precisely the missing 'geometric' hypothesis) and the identification of $\Phi^G$-mapping with genuine mapping. None of this is justified.

**To close:** (i) define 'geometric Mackey functor' character-by-character; (ii) prove the equivalence by computing $\Phi^K(N^TS^1\otimes HM)$ for all $K$; (iii) state the bracket identity as a lemma with verified hypotheses.

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Re-review: addressed in current `solution.tex`.
(i) 'Geometric Mackey functor' is now defined (Def. `def:geom`: $M(G/H)=0$ for proper $H$). (ii) The asserted equivalence $S^{|T/G|}\otimes HM\simeq N^TS^1\otimes HM$ is no longer used; the crux mapping computation `lem:geom`(3) instead uses ONLY that the **target** $\Sigma^kHM$ is $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}$-local: $[N^TS^1,\Sigma^kHM]^G\cong[\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes N^TS^1,\Sigma^kHM]^G\cong[\Phi^GN^TS^1,\Phi^G\Sigma^kHM]$ via (L1)+(L2), then $\Phi^GN^TS^1=S^{|T/G|}$ (`prop:phiNT`) and $\Phi^G\Sigma^kHM=\Sigma^kHA$ (G2). No need to check all $\Phi^K$. This gap is closed (modulo `prop:phiNT`'s own justification, tracked in q5-24).

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Review and reinforcement: gap is closed.

I reviewed the three demands of this issue against the current `solution.tex` and confirm they are all met; I additionally hardened the single most delicate step.

**(i) 'Geometric Mackey functor' is now defined.** Definition `def:geom`: $M(G/H)=0$ for every proper $H\subsetneq G$ (M1), with the (automatic) vanishing of transfers/restrictions to/from proper $H$ (M2). Equivalently $M=\underline A^{\mathrm{geo}}$ is the inflation of $A=M(G/G)$.

**(ii) The unproved equivalence $S^{|T/G|}\otimes HM\simeq N^TS^1\otimes HM$ is no longer used at all.** The issue's correct objection — that an equivalence in $\mathrm{Sp}^G$ must be checked on $\Phi^K$ for ALL $K$, not just $K=G$ — is sidestepped because no spectrum-level equivalence of this form is asserted. The crux computation `lem:geom`(3) instead computes the *mapping group* $[N^TS^1,\Sigma^kHM]^G$, and it does so using ONLY that the **target** $\Sigma^kHM$ is $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}$-local:
$$[N^TS^1,\Sigma^kHM]^G \overset{(L1)}{\cong} [\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes N^TS^1,\Sigma^kHM]^G = [\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes N^TS^1,\widetilde{E\mathcal P}\otimes\Sigma^kHM]^G \overset{(L2)}{\cong} [\Phi^GN^TS^1,\Phi^G\Sigma^kHM].$$
Both source and target of the final bracket are $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}$-local, so the localization equivalence $\Phi^G\colon L\,\mathrm{Sp}^G\xrightarrow{\sim}\mathrm{Sp}$ (L2) legitimately turns $\Phi^G$-mapping into genuine mapping. No claim is made that $[X,-]$ is detected by $\Phi^GX$ for arbitrary $X$; this is explicitly flagged in the proof.

**(iii) The bracket identity is a lemma with verified hypotheses.** It is exactly `lem:geom`(3), resting on (L1)+(L2) (recorded with proofs/standard references) and on (G1) $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}$-locality + (G2) $\Phi^GHM\simeq HA$, both proved in `prop:geomEM`. Then $\Phi^GN^TS^1\simeq S^{|T/G|}$ (`prop:phiNT`, proved in place) and $\Phi^G\Sigma^kHM=\Sigma^kHA$ give $[S^{|T/G|},\Sigma^kHA]=\pi_{|T/G|-k}HA=0$ for $|T/G|>k$.

**Hardening I added.** The one place where the 'geometric/concentration over $G/G$' hypothesis genuinely does work is (G1) ($\widetilde{E\mathcal P}$-locality). The prior text justified $i_H^\ast HM\simeq 0$ via a slightly hand-wavy 'same reasoning applied to all subgroups.' I rewrote (G1) into two clean steps:
- **(G1a)** $i_H^\ast HM\simeq H(i_H^\ast M)$ (restriction is exact and $t$-exact, sending the EM object to the EM object of the restricted Mackey functor), and $(i_H^\ast M)(H/K)=M(G/K)$. Since any $K\subseteq H\subsetneq G$ is itself proper in $G$, (M1) forces $(i_H^\ast M)(H/K)=0$ for **all** $K\subseteq H$; hence the restricted Mackey functor is identically zero and $i_H^\ast HM\simeq 0$.
- **(G1b)** $E\mathcal P_+$ is built from cells $G/K_+$ with $K$ proper; by the projection formula $G/K_+\otimes HM\simeq G_+\otimes_K i_H^\ast HM\simeq 0$, so $E\mathcal P_+\otimes HM\simeq 0$, giving $\widetilde{E\mathcal P}$-locality by (L1).

This makes the dependence on the geometric hypothesis fully explicit and removes the residual vagueness.

**Residual.** The only external dependency is `prop:phiNT` ($\Phi^G N^TS^1\simeq S^{|T/G|}$), which is itself proved in place (permutation-sphere identification $N^TS^1\cong S^{\mathbb R\cdot T}$ + $\Phi^GS^V\simeq S^{V^G}$ + $\dim(\mathbb R\cdot T)^G=|T/G|$) and tracked separately in q5-24. No circularity.

Resolving.
